column6: ALERT ALERT !  Please note that the OFFICIAL definitions of the Headword "kāryakāraṇe" END WITH the following source citation: "Pt. 1. 412".  AFTER THIS POINT – TWO ADDITIONAL MISSING COMPOUNDS FOLLOW AS: LB 1)  THE ADDITIONAL COMPOUND: " ˚bhāvaḥ" ——EXPANDING INTO——> "kāryakāraṇabhāvaḥ". This ADDITIONAL COMPOUND MUST BE LISTED INDEPENDENTLY! Therefore Please add the Compound "kāryakāraṇabhāvaḥ" to the Hierarchy List Column as a Searchable Headword, along with its related definition: "the relation of cause and effect."  LB 2)  THE ADDITIONAL COMPOUND: " ˚saṃbandhaḥ" ——EXPANDING INTO——> "kāryakāraṇasaṃbandhaḥ". This ADDITIONAL COMPOUND MUST BE LISTED INDEPENDENTLY! Therefore Please add the Compound "kāryakāraṇasaṃbandhaḥ" to the Hierarchy List Column as a Searchable Headword, along with its related definition: "The relation of cause and effect. In mīmāṃsā this is established by the rule of anvayavyatireka (i. e. tadbhāve bhāvaḥ tadabhāve abhāvaḥ) The earliest exposition of this expression is found in the śābarabhāṣya on MS. 4. 3. 2 ; cf. kāryakāraṇasambandho nāma sa bhavati, yasmin sati yad bhavati yasmiṃścāsati yanna bhavati, tatraiva kāryakāraṇasaṃbandhaḥ . This should be contrasted with the naiyāyika view of anvaya-vyatireka which they use for establishing vyāpti."  LB 3) ALSO, Please note that these TWO MISSING COMPOUNDS, must be included in the OFFICIAL LIST of Compounds given in the Window Display of the HEADWORD: "kārya" !

column7: Typo – Please note that these TWO words are clearly adjectives, as they will require a Substantive to Carry Their Stated Quality!
